channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for. — 151 replies — 148.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for.

Open thread • Replies: 151 • Updated: 2026-06-24 16:30 UTC

OP

If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for.

LINK is a $5.7B token on $57.5M of annual fees. That’s ~99x revenue, and it has been flat for years, straight through every “banks are coming” cycle. The promise was always that adoption converts into value. It hasn’t. It’s been “soon” since 2020.

So here’s the question no diehard wants to answer:

What fee number, by what date, would make you sell? If LINK still earns ~$57M in 2028, is the thesis wrong, or is it still “soon”? If you can’t name the number and the date, you’re not investing in Chainlink. You’re believing in it. And belief has no exit.

Early replies (first 3)

62382825
never. Its not about the link token, its just about the hope for the future it gives. That is what keeps me going, even if its a delusion. Thus I will never sell, because as long as I hold the token that fantastical delusion can still be real in my mind

62382842
chatgpt post

62382990
>>62382842
OP is a mentally ill faggot

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62400234
>>62400097
>samefagging fuddie with a fresh new ID complaining about linkies being in link threads

What the fuck is wrong with you.

62400297
>>62400234
>Every post that triggers me is made by the same poster.
Mind breaking heavy bags

62400329
>>62400297
You really have no idea how insanely obvious you are, do you?

62400348
>>62398292
kek
the entire thesis of Chainlink btfo in a single post

62400559
>>62400329
You are deranged lmao, its hilarious how you think you are always talking to 1 person.

62400655
>>62400559
This is a dead board, this thread has been up for almost a week now.
Yet you keep actively seeking out Link threads and then crying about all the "Link shills" you see in them. All the while pretending it's a normal thing to do.

It's not, and you stick out more than ever.

62400679
>dead board
>retarded shill keeps necro bumping his dead shill thread
>hurrrr why does the entire board shit on me?? you're all samefags1!!1!1
stop giving him (You)s and don't forget to sagepost frens

62400691
>>62400679
>shill thread
oof, might wanna check out the OP, samefagboy.

62400897
>>62400655
I just noticed you spamming the thread bieps, just hopped in. Hit you with the reminder of the flippening. You verbally shit yourself again. The regular.

62400962
>>62382813
>white male

no thanks

62401040
>>62400897
>muh flippening
we know you're a xerpie, yes

62401056
>>62400962
Russians arent white

62401136
>>62401056
niggers aren't black

62401340
>still going at it for 5 days
removing IP count was a mistake

62401653
Rome wasn't built in a day.

62401756
Didn't read never selling. Simple as.

62401863
>>62382825
This and the 4.32% roi for doing absolutely nothing

>>62383155
Tbh the only reason literal nobodies as us had a chance to stake was because we held in private wallet a during the 50$ peak, and thus could opt in as I met the onchain criteria, so due to that im happy in the end

62402855
ok, sold the rest, can't stand these anons making excuses anymore. have fun

62402860
>>62402855
Me too!
I'll post my sell order as evidence in a minute

62402872
Ada’s death spiral is glorious. At least they can all sell now and move over to xrp because how fucking stupid they are.

62403508
Imagine "investing" in OpenAI credits to try and make a profit just because they're gonna release a new model.

That's how silly buying link tokens in order to make money from new releases sounds

62403613
shut the actual fuck up and line up for the vaccine incels

62403671
>>62395706
Oh I remember this deranged pseud, he's the one who proudly claimed that he invested in the kike grifter shitcoin xrp because it represents his "Christian values" and anyone who attacks xrp is basically one of the "pharisees"

It was hard to believe that someone could be that retarded but biz is a wasteland now, and he's one of the more retarded mutants apparently

62403684
>>62403613
I am unvaxxed and I hold link

62403723
Sergey?

62403906
>>62394749
Imagine defending a guy named fucking Gensler

62404804
>>62398292
It depends on the location of the employees. How hard does McDonalds work to obsfucate the price differences between different locations?

62404810
>>62403723
No think it was some fortune top 10 guy

62405991
Money stolen by a literal fucking pajeet
People need to start doing something about these scammers. Do they plan on hosting another smart con or do they dare not face link holders?
When they stop doing smart con you know that they're finally pulling the rug

62405996
>>62405991
The pajeet fucker in question

item.title: Irl crime — 64 replies — 66.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Irl crime

Open thread • Replies: 64 • Updated: 2026-06-24 16:34 UTC

OP

Share crime stories and currently relevant opportunities ITT. IRL shit, not online scamming.

Inb4
>muh bankers muh politicians
Wow, very funny, what an original joke

Early replies (first 3)

62393319
>>62393307
I fucked your mom.

62393322
12 years ago my first job was in a supermarket. I randomly decided to steal a candy bar for no reason, but i felt so guilty that I confessed to my supervisor a day later and offered to pay. She said it was okay, and nothing happened.

62393326
>>62393319
This guy paid me $50 so he could fuck your mom.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62397565
>>62396900
Retards posting obviously fake quotes is always funny

62397959
>>62397557
Kys

62398384
>>62393307
Me on the picrel

62398550
>>62393307
>>62393356
>>62394022
>>62395812
>>62397020
>>62397565
>>62397959
Izzat farmers
Abandon thread

62398943
Bump

62399147
>>62393307
Sleep Tight Pigger

62399469
This one time my bros and i knew about 35 mil worth of gold bars so we painted the floor beneath their safe with explosives and then had a decoy boat drive off as we cracked the safe in the water below. Then we scuba'd the bars to a seperate boat, and wbile celebrating one of our own bros decided to double cross us as we celebrated our heist. Fuckin loser didnt even know what to do with all that money besides copy all our ideas. So anyway we come up with a plan to steal the gold back by sending our dead safe cracker's daughter (whom the coward was unfamiliar with) to sneak into his house disguised as a television internet repair woman, and we cut the lines of his tv. Our plans were almost ruined when his neighbors had a house party so we called it off and had to rethink our plans; we surveilled him and found out he was moving our gold in 3 trucks so we modded 3 mini coopers all souped up and pulled another demolished floor trick, the loser viewing from the helicopter couldnt see because we had a billboard truck that was blocking the view of the gold truck falling into the subway below. We knew which truck the gold was in because the ride height and our hackerbro friend basically led them right into a traffic jam above the explosive floor. After incapacitating the drivers we cracked the safe and transported the gold in our minis to a train. Our former betrayor tried to buy his way onto the train only to find that we paid off the mobsters that owned the train a ton of cash and gold to be part of our plan too. Then we listened to money by pink floyd.

62399612
>>62399469

That hackerbro's name? Albert Einstein.

62399967
>>62393322
I one time worked at Walmart a summer in high school and someone was cutting up bags to eat handfuls and I had to clean it. They didn’t give me a lunch break because I was so new and I was throwing away dozens of bags so I ate a single chip in a bag that was popped but not fully opened. Got fired on the spot as they were watching me on video like a hawk. Fuck that gay company. I’ve never bought from them since

62400055
>>62399967
Lol did you learn your retard-goy lesson?

62400082
>>62393319
Bestiality is technically illegal

62400093
I have not and will commit any crime as I am a model citizen and do not break the law.
Anything I have posted on this site that contradicts that is fairy tales and only a fool would take those such posts as fact.

62400095
>>62400093
Will NOT commit*
God fucking damn

62400096
>>62400093
Wow that's really clever and original and funny

62400920
>>62393307
comfy

62400948
>>62393307
You call that a crime scene?
Here’s a real big one bed in the street you lightweight!

62401887
Bump

62401956
This is the most pathetic fed agent trap Ive ever seen.
You guys, speaking to LE here, realize that most people on 4chan are just regular people? Maybe a little edgier and weirder? Go fuck yourself.

62401967
>>62393307
I stole some dinosaur toys from my younger cousin. My aunt and uncle obviously noticed but let it slip because they knew I was a poor kid.

Also, I guess picking up banknotes after old people drop them once they get up from public transportation seating? I have "stolen" like 20 bucks in my life that way. Why do the old and disabled retards don't give a shit about losing money, anyways?

62401994
>>62401956
>Listen here kid, you HAVE to follow arbitrary Jewish law!

62401997
I worked at my Mom's job for a Boomercare home when Covid first broke out. There was a massive shortage of nurses so a good amount of the tasks were definitely illegal since I was just some guy in zoom college and didn't have any qualifications to be anywhere near any of the patients, but it was very comfy and ironically helped me get the job I work now more than that college diploma did lol

62402026
>>62393307
>Dusty summer sky
>Eucalyptus
>Wild boar
>yellow plates
>Commie block
>Paper only recycling bins
Israel, My guess is within 250meters of downtown tel aviv
Watch yourself Lior, you are glowing.

62402123
>>62393424
The only thing he was right about tho was buying bitcoin early. You don’t need to do crime for that.

62402179
>>62401956
Yeah I'm an NSA agent trying to catch criminals using this thread.

>>62402026
Maybe the Hebrew writing would've been the clue to follow here, Detective Dipshit.
On no, looks like you've uncovered my glowie operation for Tel Aviv!


Jews are absolutely right about goyim and niggers are right about hickeys and jeets are tight about timmies.
There's a very good reason for your current predicament

62402182
>>62402179
Honkeys*
Whitoids

62403110
>62402179
Fag

62404699
Bump

62405103
i scam amazon regularly but it's peanuts. I'm thinking of becoming a full time online scammer cause i gave up on regular jobs. I have experience with coding, i just need to find a profitable avenue

62405270
>>62393307
one time when i was a kid i stole a candy bar
i felt bad so i went back and left a coin on the counter

62406004
I think I stole cigarette money from the fraternity bar thee times while serving, I also stole a packet of gum once when I was like 8, by accident.
I felt bad for a full hour which is a long time when you're 8

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-06-24 17:27 UTC

Early replies (first 3)

62405591
lol
saved for the wrecked bobo collage

62405625

62405674
>>62405591

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62405833
>>62405591
here, save this one too mumunig
We are going to 15k, anybody with 2 braincells left to rub together knows this

62405893
>>62405833
saved

62405902
>>62405301

62405908
>>62405301
I thought Saylor bought

62405910
>>62405833
30k is within the realm of possible but 15k is a legit impossibility.

62405917
>>62405833
I remember you guys saying this sort of thing last cycle, while you were simultaneously buying

62405939
>>62405917
last cycle people were calling for 8K well into the recovery phase

62405946
>>62405939
It was $12k, with the real bottom at $15k.
This time they're calling for $20k, so bottom will be around $25k.

62405951
>>62405917
They secret families/illuminati/etc aren't going to let Michael Saylor become one of the world's wealthiest man, Bitcoin will keep imploding until he is gone from the scene

62405956
>>62405939
That's why I said "something like that". They moved the target to fit this cycle, but they're still pulling the same shit

>>62405946
They were calling way lower than 12k

62405958
>>62405946
that fat guy kept calling for 8 k iirc

62406158
>>62405301
>wait 5 years to go back the same start award
Kek, even normies holding saving account has more money than you cause their money still increases despite inflation

62406273
>>62405301
>bottoms at 45k eos
check'em

item.title: Why aren't you holding BTC? — 38 replies — 42.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why aren't you holding BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 38 • Updated: 2026-06-24 16:37 UTC

OP

Too expensive? Prefer alts? Don't trust it? Or do you simply think there are better opportunities elsewhere?
Curious to hear your thoughts.

Early replies (first 3)

62395695
you can get better gains on stocks. those companies work for your money. btc sits there doing nothing but has the same risk profile as sharing a hobos needle without greater fools left to carry it to $200k

62395915
give me reasons to hold btc.

62395925
I am holding because I bought in 2020 before etfs now I refuse to sell because I'll get audit raped by the CRA supposedly so I'm stuck holding until it's got better tax laws or borrowing rates.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62399676
>>62398819
BSV is super dead super super dead

62399898
>>62395655
BTC is dead.
Western governments are all over it, they want your Capital Gains via taxes.
Unless you got in early and got out of the country with your gains early you’re fucked. Australia in its recent tax changes has hidden a trap for crypto gains, by stating any earnings made overseas in the last 12 years must be retroactively given to the government to be taxed, if you’re audited and your physical wealth exceeds your earnings, you’re fucked.
Thats outside that statute of limitations for records. It’s a dual purpose instrument- to catch people working in tax havens like Dubai who claimed they were leaving Australia forever then returned 10 years later with unexplained millions, but also to catch crypto investors who were using decentralised exchanges. The ATO has been using bots to crawl through ledgers linking transactions to addresses and piecing together expenditure and entry. If you’re caught it’s not avoidance it’s evasion.

It’s too late now but y’all should’ve moved over to XMR years ago.

62400285
>>62399898
Btc is dead? Wow. Who are you?

62400293
>>62398057
>>62398163
How!!!???

62400366
>>62395655
Worst opportunity by every metric.

62400842
>>62396879
Why do people go to the casino?

62400859
>>62400285
Epstein, Jeffrey Epstein.

62400895
>>62395655
microstrategies fucked it up. he's so maxdebt and overleveraged it's too easy to rip his company apart with the next recession and then take his btc for pennies on the dollar

62400974
>>62400895
also notice the ceo for mstr is white male. we know the results already

62401175
>>62400974
Is he gay? The way he speaks

62401180
>>62400859
Where are you hiding boss? I know you're alive

62401190
>>62400293
Oh no, stop staking your btc

62401192
I hold 3.4btc. is it enough?

62401205
>>62395655
Token not needed.

62401259
>>62395655
My IQ is >85

62402158
>>62395925
What's the tax rate there?

62402616
2x pump potential from here AT MOST, while the downside potential (probably $30k bottom) is terrible.

62402679
>>62402158
Same as USA cap gains but canada doesn't give you any incentive to hold long term. TFSA is sick though. I just treat my btc as zero so it keeps me aggressive on the risk curve with my TFSA.

62403029
>>62401192
Buy more. 100 btc

62403094
>>62400293
By native staking through protocols that prioritize self-custody. Simple.

62403095
>>62396002
>Invest only in actual products made by actual companies.
What are you investing?

62404024
>>62398163
Bank transfer? Hmmm

62404122
If you're not holding BTC when it is clear that the fiat system is on its last days you are beyond retarded.

62404297
>>62395655

Big Thai Cock?
Well I mean like why aren't YOU holding it?
I mean.

62404304
>why aren't you holding goy gold
/biz/ is already out of crypto.
https://web.archive.org/web/202602162103...f-bitcoin/

62404487
>>62404304
Lol

62404614
>>62404122
Why do you need fiat?

62405784
>>62401190
Why though? If you can earn from your BTC while maintaining self-custody, I don't see the problem.

62405818
>>62398819
If it's still BTC and I still control the keys, I don't see why it stops being Bitcoin.

62406014
>>62395655
>Why aren't you holding BTC?
I will buy when it's cheaper. 100 bucks seems like a fair value for the future of finance.

item.title: BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!!! — 20 replies — 39.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!!!

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-06-24 17:01 UTC

OP

>Today, Chainlink, FairSquareLab, a first-mover in core infrastructure within the Korean digital asset ecosystem, UniKA (Unified Korea Alliance), which represents the Korean coalition behind Project Pangea and is comprised of a steering committee of 5 entities (Shinhan Bank, JB Bank, Kbank, FairSquareLab, and OBDIA) along with 10+ participating Korean commercial banks, and Qivalis, a euro stablecoin consortium powered by 37 leading European banks, announced Project Pangea, a strategic, collaborative task force focused on redefining global foreign exchange markets by unlocking real-time, stablecoin-based cross-border settlement models.


https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/chainlink-and-multinational-banking-consortia-launch-project-pangea-to-develop-t0-settlement-framework-for-international-fx-markets-302807910.html

Early replies (first 3)

62401710
Wow I can’t wait for this to not mean anything.

62401722
p+c etc.
don't care until the needle moves on this FUCKING GODDAMN TOKEN

62401738
>>62401704
xrp sneeded?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62401761
>>62401710
>>62401722
>>62401738
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!!!

62401772
I'VE JUST ABOUT HAD IT WITH THESE FUCKING ANNOUNCEMENTS

62401794
Upon reading the article it is pretty cool. I just have yet to see any of this come to fruition yet.

62402802
Bump

62402807
>>62401710
Kek FPBP
Token not needed

62402814

>Price still in the single digits
>More than half the country is brown
>Gas prices still high
don't care nothing matters until these 4 things change

62402824
>>62401738

62402858

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=26N4u0ihIT4&t=12s

62402941
>>62401704

62403111
>>62402824
Nice flippening bqggie

62403136
So bullish the chainlink

62403302
>>62401704
So that's why we are pumping.

62403523
>>62401704

62404948

62405534

62405567

62406112

>>62405801
Gold reserves are used to refill oil reserves.
Long term the gold reserves will be rebuild even more harder.
Also rate expectations.

62405834
>>62405801
Thank you for playing.

62405886
Expected last selloff in line with previous gold/silver repricing events. Silver will bottom out at $55

62405925

62406003

62406220

Because this economy is fake and gay and all it takes to dump the price of The fountain of eternal youth to shit, is the simple press of a single red button

62406236

item.title: Binance out of the EU? — 23 replies — 14.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Binance out of the EU?

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-06-24 15:55 UTC

OP

How is this going to affect the crypto market?

Early replies (first 3)

62404563
>>62404556
>the crypto market
what?

you thought theres still a market?

lol

why you think every pump gets sold off?

underwater MMs are slowly offloading to $0 into ever-decreasing demand thats your market

62404564
>>62404556
why are you still talking about crypto even? Everyone with a brain has moved onto stocks

62404566
>>62404563
MMs? Market makers? Dont those cunts make their money out of the spread?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62404567
>>62404564
I still have a bunch of usdt and there are things like staking, gold stablecoins and spacex gambling.

62404608
>>62404556
peepoo ll hold their own coins and slimy cz wont be able to manipulate the prices so easily.

62404611
>>62404556
see how dast the lil droids sliding the thred, it fears :D

62404615
>>62404556
Anyone here still using binance?

62404622
>>62404556
What kind of idiot uses Binance?

62404679
>>62404622
It seems that there are a lot of idiots anon, is the biggest exchange in the world.

62404688
>>62404622
Whats the better alternative? And please dont tell mot robinhood or some shit

62404733
>>62404556
That could cause a dump

62404795
>>62404679
They spoof volume, if they had the same regs they'd be smaller than coinbase

62404802
>>62404795
They operate in europe so they have the same regulations than coinbase, they are the biggest one because they operate on the most regions

62404814
>>62404802
This thread is about them not operating in Europe anymore because they dont meet the regs

62404825
>>62404814
The things is that this could cause a massive withdrawal, do they really have your coins in there?

62404832
>>62404814
Well they still are, no official statement was issued to users yet

62404835
>>62404825
Im not too worried about it, its been very telegraphed and binance has handled stuff like this before without much issue

Its much more lucrative for them to keep collecting fees rather than rug

62404874
>>62404688
Dexes. Only two limitations come to my mind: 1) can be hard/expensive to trade fiat->crypto and 2) you can't get "physical delivery" of dumb chain coins like BTC or LTC that don't have trustless bridges to smart chains. For everything else dexes are just better.

62405781
>>62404556
it's only temporary as long as they don't have their licence

62405795
>>62404874
dexes suck, only order books matter for trading and the volume is low, exchanges have more funds to get, that's without accounting the fact that they rely on crypto which is not needed, crypto is not replacing centralized servers

62405831
>>62404556
Europeans are poor, hence europoor. They barely have money to invest. Sometimes I think I'm blessed that my German Borderline ex-wife divorced me, imagine living in a country where you barely can make ends meet AND life alongside jeets, sandniggers and kikes that sabotage a whole continent.

62405835
>>62405831
Just so sum up:
>How is this going to affect the crypto market?
It won't.

62405842
CZ will finally use his Samson Option.
October 10th was just the appetizer.

item.title: These companies are too big to fail — 6 replies — 10.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
These companies are too big to fail

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-06-24 16:58 UTC

OP

At this point, these companies literally are the US economy. Trump, and any future president will never let them fail. Their stocks are like bonds but with more upside.
